title: quercus franchetii
intro: Quercus franchetii, also known as the Chinese corkbark oak, is a species of evergreen oak tree native to China and the Himalayan foothills. The bark of this plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus franchetii include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Quercus franchetii is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
